Libraries and SDKs that let agents hold funds, sign transactions, and move money (crypto or fiat). Split into agent payment rails and agent wallets. Examples: GOAT, Coinbase AgentKit. Distinct from Identity protocols like AP2, which define the handshake rather than the execution.

docs.title: Subcategories
docs.text:
• Agent Payment Rails (3 proj) — Payment rails and protocols that let agents pay for services, APIs, or other agents programmatically.
  • Agent Wallets (3 proj) — Wallet infrastructure giving agents custody, signing, and balance management for on-chain or fiat funds.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• AgentKit
  • GOAT

docs.title: Agent Payment Rails
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Financial Tools / Agent Payment Rails

Payment rails and protocols that let agents pay for services, APIs, or other agents programmatically.

Machine-payable infrastructure — settlement protocols, metering, and HTTP-native payment specs for agents. In scope: x402, Skyfire, Nevermined. Out of scope: agent wallets that hold funds and traditional payment APIs not agent-oriented.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• Nevermined Payments
  • Skyfire
  • x402

docs.title: Agent Wallets
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Financial Tools / Agent Wallets

Wallet infrastructure giving agents custody, signing, and balance management for on-chain or fiat funds.

Server-side and embedded wallets provisioned per agent to hold keys, sign transactions, and manage balances. In scope: Coinbase CDP AgentKit, Crossmint, Privy server wallets. Out of scope: payment rails/settlement protocols and general consumer wallets.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• Coinbase CDP AgentKit Wallets
  • Crossmint Agent Wallets
  • Privy Server Wallets

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Integrations

Generic integration/connector libraries exposing many third-party APIs as agent-callable tools.

SDKs that bundle hundreds of SaaS API connectors into one tool surface for agents (Composio). Distinct from Authenticated Tool Proxies in that the focus is the connector catalog and SDK shape, not managed auth-as-a-service.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • Composio

docs.title: Marketplaces
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Marketplaces

Marketplaces where agents and their actions are listed, discovered, and transacted by other agents or humans.

Directories and storefronts for publishing or hiring agents and packaged actions. In scope: agent listing sites, discovery portals, commerce layers for agent services. Out of scope: MCP registries (see MCP > Registries & Marketplaces) and tool integration catalogs.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• agen.cy Marketplace
  • AI Agent Store

docs.title: Software Extensions
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Software Extensions

Extensions that embed agentic actions into existing host software such as IDEs, notebooks, or editors.

Plugins and add-ons that expose agent capabilities inside a host application rather than standing alone. In scope: notebook assistants, IDE extensions, editor plugins delivering tool-call actions. Out of scope: standalone agents and browser/computer-use runtimes.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • Jupyter AI

docs.title: Upserting Tools
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Upserting Tools

Tools that let agents insert or update records in external systems (CRMs, databases, SaaS) as a write-side action.

The write counterpart to extraction: action tools specialized in upserting structured data back into systems of record. In scope: CRM/DB upsert connectors and writeback utilities exposed to agents. Out of scope: read-only data extraction and general integration catalogs.

docs.title: Voice
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Voice

Voice action tools letting agents place calls, speak, and handle telephony-grade audio interactions.

Libraries and services that give agents voice as an action surface — outbound/inbound calls, IVR, real-time audio. In scope: voice agent SDKs and telephony runtimes used as tools. Out of scope: voice-first agent frameworks (Frameworks > Voice) and raw TTS/STT models.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • Bolna

docs.title: Web Search APIs for Agents
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Actions / Web Search APIs for Agents

Web search APIs purpose-built as agent tool calls, returning LLM-friendly results rather than SERP HTML.

Search-as-a-service endpoints designed for agent consumption: clean snippets, ranked passages, and retrieval-oriented responses. In scope: Tavily, Exa, Linkup, Serper and similar. Out of scope: general scraping, browser automation, and vector retrieval over private corpora.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• Exa
  • Linkup
  • Serper
  • Tavily

docs.title: MCP
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / MCP

Model Context Protocol ecosystem — servers, gateways, aggregators, registries, and third-party MCP implementations.

Everything in and around MCP, the emerging standard for exposing tools and context to LLMs. Covers server implementations, gateways that multiplex or proxy MCP traffic, aggregation layers, and marketplaces. Distinct from generic tool libraries — these are specifically MCP-shaped.

docs.title: Subcategories
docs.text:
• Aggregation (1 proj) — Services that aggregate many MCP servers behind a single unified interface for an agent client.
  • Gateways (1 proj) — MCP gateways that proxy, translate, or policy-enforce between MCP clients and backend servers.
  • Registries & Marketplaces (3 proj) — Registries and marketplaces for discovering, publishing, and installing MCP servers.
  • Third Party (1 proj) — Third-party MCP servers wrapping external products or APIs that the vendor itself does not ship.

docs.title: Aggregation
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / MCP / Aggregation

Services that aggregate many MCP servers behind a single unified interface for an agent client.

Aggregators fan out a client's MCP connection across multiple backend servers, multiplexing tools into one session. In scope: multi-server MCP hosts and bundlers. Out of scope: protocol gateways that translate transports, and registries that only list servers.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • Klavis

docs.title: Gateways
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / MCP / Gateways

MCP gateways that proxy, translate, or policy-enforce between MCP clients and backend servers.

Middleware sitting on the wire between MCP client and server — handling transport translation, auth, routing, observability, and policy. In scope: MCP proxies and context-forge style gateways. Out of scope: pure aggregators and server registries.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • MCP Context Forge

docs.title: Registries & Marketplaces
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / MCP / Registries & Marketplaces

Registries and marketplaces for discovering, publishing, and installing MCP servers.

Catalogs of MCP servers with metadata, install flows, and sometimes hosted execution. In scope: Smithery, PulseMCP, the official MCP Registry. Out of scope: gateways and aggregators that run servers, and non-MCP agent/action marketplaces.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• MCP Registry (official)
  • PulseMCP
  • Smithery

docs.title: Third Party
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / MCP / Third Party

Third-party MCP servers wrapping external products or APIs that the vendor itself does not ship.

Community-built MCP servers exposing SaaS products, websites, or APIs as tools when no first-party server exists. In scope: independently maintained server implementations for specific services. Out of scope: official/vendor MCP servers and registries listing them.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: • MCP Server Airbnb

docs.title: Standards
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Standards

Open standards and specifications for agent interoperability — ratified, proposed, and ecosystem-tied protocols.

Specification-level artifacts defining how agents, tools, and services interoperate. Segmented by maturity: ratified standards, proposals under discussion, and emerging standards tightly coupled to a single vendor's ecosystem. Distinct from MCP, which has its own dedicated branch.

docs.title: Subcategories
docs.text:
• Emerging (Ecosystem-Tied) (2 proj) — Emerging agent standards implemented inside a specific ecosystem or product rather than a neutral body.
  • Proposed (2 proj) — Proposed agent-ecosystem standards still in draft — not yet widely implemented or ratified.
  • Ratified (3 proj) — Ratified, broadly adopted agent interoperability protocols backed by multi-vendor implementations.

docs.title: Emerging (Ecosystem-Tied)
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Standards / Emerging (Ecosystem-Tied)

Emerging agent standards implemented inside a specific ecosystem or product rather than a neutral body.

Specs gaining traction through a particular platform or browser vendor before any cross-industry ratification. In scope: WebMCP and similar ecosystem-anchored proposals. Out of scope: fully ratified cross-vendor protocols and early paper-only proposals.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• WebMCP
  • WebMCP (Chrome EPP)

docs.title: Proposed
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Standards / Proposed

Proposed agent-ecosystem standards still in draft — not yet widely implemented or ratified.

Early-stage specifications circulated for feedback: protocols, certifications, or interoperability drafts without committed adopters yet. In scope: draft specs like GitAgent or AIUC-1. Out of scope: ratified protocols and specs already shipping inside a specific ecosystem.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• AIUC-1
  • GitAgent

docs.title: Ratified
docs.text:
Tools & Integrations / Standards / Ratified

Ratified, broadly adopted agent interoperability protocols backed by multi-vendor implementations.

Published specs with stable versions and shipping implementations across the ecosystem. In scope: MCP, A2A, AG-UI and similar. Out of scope: draft proposals and ecosystem-tied emerging specs.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
• A2A (Agent2Agent Protocol)
  • AG-UI (Agent User Interaction Protocol)
  • MCP (Model Context Protocol)
